advisories.title: Exec Approval Bypass via chat.send -> /approve
advisories.description: A gateway client with only operator.write scope could approve or deny exec approvals by sending /approve or /deny through chat.send, bypassing the required operator.approvals scope. This undermines the entire manual execution approval security model.

advisories.title: Origin Bypass via Trusted-Proxy Mode
advisories.description: WebSocket connections bypass origin validation when gateway.auth.mode is set to trusted-proxy and proxy headers are present. An attacker-controlled page can inherit the proxy-authenticated identity to perform privileged actions.

advisories.title: 1-Click Remote Code Execution (RCE)
advisories.description: OpenClaw UI and Gateway trusted the gatewayUrl parameter from query strings without validation. Clicking a malicious link could exfiltrate auth tokens or allow RCE by modifying tool policies.

advisories.title: macOS SSH Command Injection
advisories.description: Improper sanitization of SSH-related inputs on macOS allowed for arbitrary command injection during remote discovery operations.

advisories.title: Docker Sandbox Escape
advisories.description: Vulnerability in the default Docker sandbox configuration allowed agents to escape the container boundary and access the host filesystem.

advisories.title: Prompt Injection via Workspace Path
advisories.description: Crafted workspace directory names could be used to inject instructions into the LLM prompt during initialization, potentially leading to unauthorized actions.

advisories.title: SSRF in Gateway Image Tool
advisories.description: The built-in image processing tool allowed Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), enabling attackers to probe internal networks through the gateway.

advisories.title: Path Traversal in Browser Upload
advisories.description: Browser upload functionality failed to properly sanitize filenames, allowing arbitrary file writes to the host filesystem via path traversal (..).

advisories.title: Skills.status Secret Disclosure
advisories.description: The skills.status API endpoint accidentally included sensitive environment variables and credentials in its JSON response.

advisories.title: Zalo Webhook Rate Limit Bypass
advisories.description: Rate limiting was applied only after authentication. This allowed brute-force attacks against the Zalo webhook secret.

advisories.title: BlueBubbles Webhook Auth Bypass
advisories.description: BlueBubbles webhook handler treated all requests from loopback as authenticated, allowing bypass when behind a reverse proxy.

advisories.title: Exec Approval Bypass via Glob Pattern Overmatching
advisories.description: Flawed glob-to-POSIX translation logic in exec-approvals allowed attackers to execute unauthorized system commands by crafting paths that bypassed allowlist patterns.

advisories.title: Credential Exposure in Setup Codes
advisories.description: Device pairing mechanism embedded long-lived shared credentials in setup code payloads, allowing attackers to gain persistent access if codes were intercepted.
